title: User Identity and Tenant Membership Semantic Contract
description: Draft semantic contract for user identity, external identity linkage, tenant membership, and session actor projection required by the authentication first slice. The first implementation slice requires user identity to be projected only through verified tenant membership and to fail closed for absent or other-tenant membership.
aiGuidance: Treat user identity and membership as semantic prerequisites for authenticated session context. Do not infer membership rules from implementation structure alone.

lifecycle.description: Membership lifecycle is explicit: an invite validates and creates either a pending invitation for a new user or a direct membership for an existing user, an invited membership becomes active on first login, role/status/username can be updated, a disabled membership can be re-enabled, and removal deletes the membership. Each transition is gated by precondition reads before any change.

stateModel.description: State is a tenant-scoped membership carrying user id, organisation id, tenant role, optional username, status (invited, active, or disabled), creation/update/last-login timestamps, and inviter, alongside separate pending-invitation rows. Membership is always scoped to one organisation.

permissions.description: Routes require the specific member read, invite, update-role, or delete permission, and the manager role intentionally holds only a legacy member bundle without the tenant member-management permissions. All reads and writes derive the tenant from resolved context, never from client input, preserving tenant isolation.

contracts.description: The operation surface lists members, invites a member, updates or deletes a member, updates username or status, resends an invite, and reads external identities. Mutations return typed results mapped to standard success and error responses.

validation.description: Email is format-validated and lowercased, system-admin is rejected as a tenant membership role, username format and status transitions are explicit, and the last tenant-admin cannot be demoted, disabled, or removed. Invalid input is rejected before any mutation.

errorModel.description: Typed outcomes include invalid-body, conflict, already-invited, not-found, the three last-admin guards, invalid-transition, and ok, mapped fail-closed to 400/409/404/422/204/201 responses; provider failure returns a typed degraded result and keeps mutation boundaries closed. Conflict paths emit no misleading state.

auditModel.description: Invite, role change, username/status change, resend-invite, and remove emit audit only after validation and precondition reads, audit failure aborts the mutation, and conflict paths emit no misleading audit. Audit precedes the membership change.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is invariant-ready once tenant context and the identity store are available, with an admin-count readiness signal, and degraded mode disables store-dependent mutation while keeping read-only listing and audit visible. Pending-invitation queries always filter by organisation id.

proof.description: Unit and substrate proofs cover the membership usecase and its v2 variant, the identity domain, the identity repository, and organisation profile and member route coverage, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic state-machine and permission validation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.
